section_title: An act done by one person on behalf of another, without such other
content:
200. An act done by one person on behalf of another, without such other
of
person's authority, which, if done with authority, would have the effect of
unauthorized
act cannot subjecting a third person to damages, or of terminating any right or interest of
injure third
a third person, cannot, by ratification, be made to have such effect.
person
Illustrations
(a) A, not being authorized thereto by B, demands, on behalf of B, the
delivery of a chattle, the property of B, from C, who is in possession of it.
This demand cannot be ratified by B, so as to make C liable for damages for
his refusal to deliver.
(b) A holds a lease from B, terminable on three months' notice. C, an
unauthorized person, gives notice of termination to A. The notice cannot be
ratified by B, so as to be binding on A.
Revocation of Authority
Termination
